claim: Gemma 4 uses per-layer embedding architecture to reduce active parameters (2B of 5B) while maintaining quality, enabling on-device inference on phones and Raspberry Pi
actionable: For on-device deployments, consider models using parameter offloading architecture; only a fraction of parameters must be in GPU memory, rest can stay on CPU/disk
source_title: Google's Open AI Strategy — Omar Sanseviero, Google DeepMind

claim: Small models (2B-4B) can now perform agentic tasks (function calling, system instructions, conversational) but struggle with knowledge retrieval; use larger models for factual reasoning
actionable: For agents: use small models on-device for tool calling and workflow orchestration, but pair with larger models or RAG for knowledge-heavy tasks requiring factuality
source_title: Google's Open AI Strategy — Omar Sanseviero, Google DeepMind

claim: Fine-tuning excitement has waned as base models improved; most conversational behavior can now be achieved via prompting, not fine-tuning
actionable: Before fine-tuning, experiment extensively with prompting and in-context examples; if models work well out-of-box, skip fine-tuning; reserve fine-tuning for domain-specific knowledge
source_title: Google's Open AI Strategy — Omar Sanseviero, Google DeepMind

claim: Sparse MoE architectures are harder to fine-tune than dense models due to routing issues and distribution shifts; dense models remain more reliable for custom training
actionable: When planning fine-tuning, prefer dense model architectures; if using MoE, expect routing complications and experiment with freezing/unfreezing experts and adjusting hyperparameters
source_title: Google's Open AI Strategy — Omar Sanseviero, Google DeepMind

claim: Diffusion models for text generation are faster than autoregressive but currently lower quality; useful for code generation and fill-in-middle but not a general replacement yet
actionable: For speed-critical code generation, experiment with diffusion-based text generators; they excel at parallel decoding but may sacrifice output quality on complex reasoning
source_title: Google's Open AI Strategy — Omar Sanseviero, Google DeepMind

claim: Mechanistic interpretability (GemmaScope) enables engineers to understand model activations per layer and per token, making research more accessible to applied engineers
actionable: Use GemmaScope or similar interpretability tools to inspect model behavior on specific tokens/layers, reducing mysterious failures in agent behavior
source_title: Google's Open AI Strategy — Omar Sanseviero, Google DeepMind

claim: Researchers increasingly use agentic tools for ablations and experiments; building research-friendly agentic tooling accelerates both research and engineering
actionable: Create agentic tools for running model experiments and ablations; this enables faster iteration for both researchers and engineers on the same codebase
source_title: Google's Open AI Strategy — Omar Sanseviero, Google DeepMind

claim: Multi-LoRA strategies for on-device models face infrastructure challenges; updating a base model requires updating many LoRAs, creating versioning and storage problems
actionable: Avoid shipping multiple task-specific LoRAs on-device; instead, use a single base model with prompting or in-context learning for task adaptation
source_title: Google's Open AI Strategy — Omar Sanseviero, Google DeepMind
